methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.1B - $39.2B.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $39.2B

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $2.26B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.09B

data.narrative:
Credo Semiconductor, a public-stage chip and semiconductor company founded by William Brennan and Lawrence Cheng, commands a current valuation of approximately $35.8B following its 2022 IPO. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage public peers within Chips & Semiconductors, alongside an extended dataset of 8 private frontier-tech companies in adjacent areas.

The peer-median approach, the applicable triangulation method, references valuations for Cerebras ($39.2B), Ouster ($2.3B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.1B). The median across this set stands at $2.3B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $39.2B. This method yields an implied valuation of $2.3B for a comparable-stage, same-sector company.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.6B, centered on $2.3B. However, material uncertainty exists regarding the applicability of the peer set: the wide dispersion ($1.1B–$39.2B) reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, addressable market size, and commercialization progress within the semiconductor frontier-tech space. Credo's current $35.8B valuation represents significant optionality priced in relative to median-comparable valuation, likely reflecting investor expectations around specific technical capability or market position not captured in this static peer sample. The single-method triangulation constrains analytical depth.
